bodyHtml:
Home/FHA Loan Limits Florida
FHA Loan Limits Florida

FHA loan limits in Florida (2026)

The 2026 FHA loan and mortgage limits in Florida range from a statewide floor of $541,287 to $990,150 in Monroe County. Here's the full county-by-county breakdown by property type.

2026 Figures

Florida FHA loan limits by county for 2026

FHA loan limits are set every year by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) and vary by county based on local home prices. For 2026, the single-family FHA loan limit in Florida starts at a statewide floor of $541,287 and rises to $990,150 in the highest-cost county.

Limits apply to FHA case numbers assigned on or after January 1, 2026. The national high-cost ceiling is $1,249,125. A small number of Florida counties fall between the floor and these figures — always verify your exact county limit using HUD's official FHA loan limit lookup before making an offer.

How FHA loan limits work in Florida

FHA mortgage limits are calculated as a percentage of the conforming loan limit set by the Federal Housing Finance Agency. The FHA “floor” equals 65% of the conforming limit and applies to lower-cost counties, while the “ceiling” is set at 150% and applies to the most expensive metro areas.

Because most Florida counties have median home prices below the high-cost threshold, the majority sit at the $541,287 floor. High-cost coastal markets — Monroe, Collier, and the tri-county South Florida region — qualify for higher limits that reflect their pricier housing.

What if my home costs more than the FHA limit?

If the loan amount you need is above your county's FHA limit, you can make a larger down payment to bring the loan under the cap, choose a home within the limit, or consider a conventional or jumbo loan instead. We can walk you through every option.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Florida for 2026?+
For 2026, single-family FHA loan limits in Florida range from $541,287 in most counties up to $990,150 in Monroe County. Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach are above the $541,287 floor and Collier (Naples) is above the $541,287 floor.
Which Florida county has the highest FHA loan limit?+
Monroe County, home to the Florida Keys and Key West, has the highest FHA loan limit in Florida at $990,150 for a single-family home in 2026.
Do FHA loan limits change every year?+
Yes. HUD reviews and adjusts FHA loan limits annually based on home price movements. The 2026 floor rose to $541,287 from $524,225 in 2025.
Are FHA limits higher for multi-unit properties?+
Yes. FHA loan limits increase with the number of units. In most Florida counties the 2026 limit is $541,287 for one unit and rises to $1,041,125 for a four-unit property.
